Fnrn Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CouvrrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramenro, CA 95g1.1-2329
\916) 322-5660 r Fax (9t6) 322_0886
July 19,2010
Ms. Andriette Culbertson
Culbertson, Adams and Associates
1975 Still Meadow Road
Ballard, Califomia 93460
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-10-104
Dear Ms. Culbertson:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Section 8430g of the political
Reform Act (the "Act").' This letter is based on the facti presented. The Fair political practices
Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it rendeis assistance. (In re Oglesby ( 1975) I
FPPC ops' 71.) Because yolr qtlestion is general in nature, we are treating yollr request as one
flor informal assistance.j
QUESTION
Pursuant to Section 84308, would a contribution by your spolrse, made out of either a
joint or community property accollnt, reduce your ability to contribute to the same candidate?
CONCLUSION
So long as the proceeding does not involve property or a bnsiness that is owned jointly by
the husband and wife, and so long as the spouse is not acting as the agent of the party or
participant spouse, the contributions of the husband and wife will noibe aggregaled fo. p,,,.por".
of disqualification under Section 84308 and the nonparty/participant ,po,,i"ir |ot subject to the
limit in Section 84308.
I The Political Refbrm Act is ctrntained in covernment Code Sections ti l(x)0 through 91014. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections lti I l0 through I t1997 of 'title 2 of thi Caliturnia Cotle of Re-uulations. r\ll
regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherrvise indicated.
I Infirrrnal assistance dtles not provide the requestur w'ith the immunity pr.ovided by an gpinion 0r tormal
written advice. (Section tii l r.r, Regulati.n rg329tc)tj), copy encr,sed.l
6;r-


Our File No. I-10-104
Page2
FACTS
You are a professional urban planer and attorney licensed in California and the sole
owner of your company, Cr-rlbertson, Adams and Associates, Inc. You have appeared before the
Califomia Coastal Commission for over 25 years. You represent governments such as cities and
counties, as well as private landowners. Your work is aimed at compliance with the Coastal Act.
You spouse was your business partner from 1980. He is currently not involved in your
company or any of its representations. He does not attend Coastal Commission meetings and has
no contact with the Commission, its staff, or your clients.
You wish to know if your husband is limited, as you are, from contributing to persons
serving on the Coastal Commission but running for public office. Your husband knows certain
of the commissioners on a social basis and would like to participate more fully in the campaign
contribution process.
ANALYSIS
Section 84308 provides:
"(b) No officer of an agency shall accept, solicit, or direct a contribution
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party, or his or her agent,
or from any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a
license, permit, or other entitlement for use is pending before it " og"n.y and for
three months following the date a final decision is rendered in the froceeding if
the officer knows or has reason to know that the participant has a financial
interest, as that term is used in Article 1 (commencing with Section g7100) of
Chapter 7. This prohibition shall apply regardless of whether the officer accepts,
solicits, or directs the contribution for himself or herself, or on behalf of any ither
officer, or on behalf of any candidate for office or on behalf of any commitiee.
"(c) Prior to rendering any decision in a proceeding involving a license,
permit or other entitlement for use pending before an agency, each officer of the
agency who received a contribution within the preceding 12 months in an amount
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from a party or from any participant
shall disclose that fact on the record of the proceeding. No officer or anigency
shall make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to use his or trer officiat
position to influence the decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or
other entitlement for use pending before the agency if the officer has willfully or
knowingly received a contribution in an amollnt of more than two hundred fiity
dollars ($2SO; within the preceding L2 months from a party or his or her agent, or
from any participant, or his or her agent if the officer knows or has ,.oron1o
know that the participant has a financial interest in the decision. as that term is


Our File No. I-10-104
page 3
described with respect to public officials in Article 1 (commencing with Section
87100) of Chaprer 7."
ln this case, yoll ask if the spouse of a party in a proceeding before an agency can give
$250 to an officer of the agency or will the spouse also be subjecr io the $250 l;nit. In geieral, a
husband and wife's contributions are not aggregated for p,,.por", of disqualification under
Section 84308. (See also, Section 85308(a).) However,ihere are two scenarios where their
contributions must be aggregated for pllrposes of section g430g.
o If the proceeding involves property or a business that is owned jointly by the husband and
wife, their contributions must be aggregated for purposes of Seition b+jos. (Kymmerer
Advice Letter, No. I-89-632.)
o The spouse is acting as the agent of the party or participant spouse. Regulation 1g43g.3.
provides:
"(a) For purposes of Government Code Section 84308, a person is the
'agent' of a party to, or a participant in, a proceeding involving a iicense, permit
or other entitlement for use only if he or she ."pr".errts that peison in connection
with the proceeding involving the license, permit or other entitlement for use. [f
an individual acting as an agent is also acting as an employee or member of a law,
architectural, engineering or consulting firm, or a similar intity or corporation,
both the entity or corporation and the individual are .agents.,
"(b) To determine whether a contribution of more than $ 250 has been
made by a person or his or her agent, contributions made by that person within the
preceding 12 months shall be aggregated with those made by his tr her agent
within the preceding )2 months or the period of the agency ielationship,
whichever is shorter."
So long as the proceeding does not involve property or a business that is owned jointly by
the husband and wife, and so long as the spouse is not acting as the agent of the party or
participant spollse, the contributions of the husband and wife will noibe aggregated io. pr,rpor",
of disqualification under Section 84308 and the nonparty/participant rpou.-"-iriot subject to the
limit in Section 84308.


Our File No. I-10-104
Page No.4
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
